query:
-- MATH-3: Statistics (15 questions)
INSERT INTO bridge_academy_practice_questions (topicId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, explanation, difficulty, variationGroup) VALUES
(3, 'What is the mean of 2, 4, 6, 8, 10?', 'multiple_choice', '["4", "5", "6", "8"]', '6', 'Mean = (2+4+6+8+10)/5 = 30/5 = 6', 'easy', 'topic_3_group_0'),
(3, 'What is the median of 3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6?', 'multiple_choice', '["3.5", "4", "4.5", "5"]', '3.5', 'Ordered: 1,1,2,3,4,5,6,9. Median = (3+4)/2 = 3.5', 'medium', 'topic_3_group_0'),
(3, 'What is the mode of 2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5, 5?', 'multiple_choice', '["2", "3", "4", "5"]', '3', 'Mode is the most frequent value: 3 appears 3 times', 'easy', 'topic_3_group_1'),
(3, 'What is the range of 5, 12, 8, 3, 15?', 'multiple_choice', '["3", "8", "12", "15"]', '12', 'Range = max - min = 15 - 3 = 12', 'easy', 'topic_3_group_1'),
(3, 'If the mean of 5 numbers is 10, what is their sum?', 'multiple_choice', '["5", "10", "25", "50"]', '50', 'Sum = mean × count = 10 × 5 = 50', 'easy', 'topic_3_group_2'),
(3, 'What is the standard deviation used for?', 'multiple_choice', '["Finding the mean", "Measuring spread of data", "Finding the median", "Counting values"]', 'Measuring spread of data', 'Standard deviation measures how spread out data is from the mean', 'medium', 'topic_3_group_2'),
(3, 'What is the probability of rolling a 3 on a standard die?', 'multiple_choice', '["1/6", "1/3", "1/2", "1/4"]', '1/6', 'A standard die has 6 faces, so P(3) = 1/6', 'easy', 'topic_3_group_3'),
(3, 'If you flip a coin twice, what is the probability of getting two heads?', 'multiple_choice', '["1/2", "1/3", "1/4", "1/8"]', '1/4', 'P(HH) = P(H) × P(H) = 1/2 × 1/2 = 1/4', 'medium', 'topic_3_group_3'),
(3, 'What is the quartile that divides data in half?', 'multiple_choice', '["Q1", "Q2 (Median)", "Q3", "Q4"]', 'Q2 (Median)', 'The second quartile (Q2) is the median', 'medium', 'topic_3_group_4'),
(3, 'What does a z-score of 0 indicate?', 'multiple_choice', '["Below average", "Average (at the mean)", "Above average", "Outlier"]', 'Average (at the mean)', 'A z-score of 0 means the value equals the mean', 'hard', 'topic_3_group_4'),
(3, 'What is the interquartile range (IQR)?', 'multiple_choice', '["Q1 - Q3", "Q3 - Q1", "Q2 - Q1", "Max - Min"]', 'Q3 - Q1', 'IQR = Q3 - Q1, measuring the middle 50% of data', 'hard', 'topic_3_group_5'),
(3, 'If a dataset has values 2, 4, 6, 8, what is the variance?', 'multiple_choice', '["2", "4", "5", "6"]', '5', 'Mean = 5, Variance = [(2-5)² + (4-5)² + (6-5)² + (8-5)²]/4 = 20/4 = 5', 'hard', 'topic_3_group_5'),
(3, 'What is a normal distribution?', 'multiple_choice', '["Skewed data", "Bell-shaped curve", "Uniform distribution", "Bimodal distribution"]', 'Bell-shaped curve', 'A normal distribution is symmetric and bell-shaped', 'medium', 'topic_3_group_6'),
(3, 'What percentage of data falls within 1 standard deviation in a normal distribution?', 'multiple_choice', '["68%", "95%", "99.7%", "50%"]', '68%', 'Approximately 68% of data falls within 1 standard deviation', 'hard', 'topic_3_group_6'),
(3, 'What is a correlation coefficient?', 'multiple_choice', '["A type of average", "Measure of relationship between variables", "Standard deviation", "Probability"]', 'Measure of relationship between variables', 'Correlation coefficient measures the strength of linear relationship', 'hard', 'topic_3_group_7');
